question_text: Which diagram best shows the magnetic field pattern around a bar magnet?

options.label: A
options.text: Field lines going in random directions

options.label: B
options.text: Field lines flowing from north pole to south pole

options.label: C
options.text: Field lines only at the surface of the magnet

options.label: D
options.text: Field lines spiralling around the magnet

correct_answer: Field lines flowing from north pole to south pole
explanation: Magnetic field lines always flow from the north pole to the south pole of a magnet. This is true both outside the magnet (which we can see) and inside it. The field lines show the direction and strength of the magnetic field - they are closer together where the field is stronger (near the poles) and spread out further away.
